character_identity_card: PROTAGONIST IDENTITY (IMMUTABLE — SINGLE AGE, NO EVOLUTION, render exactly ONE figure embedded in the narrative scene described below — NEVER produce a model sheet or character lineup, NEVER show multiple ages or outfits side by side): contemporary French man, 35-40 years old, lean average build. ABSOLUTE TATTOO BAN: the protagonist has ZERO tattoos, ZERO body art, ZERO ink, ZERO tribal patterns, ZERO decorative motifs of any kind, anywhere — not on arms, not on forearms, not on hands, not on neck, not on chest, not on back, not on any visible skin or any visible part of his clothing. His skin (mitten hands and any visible patch of skin) is always pure flat white. His clothing is always plain solid colour with no decorative pattern on the sleeves or anywhere else. NEVER any irezumi, NEVER any sleeve tattoo, NEVER any tribal forearm pattern, NEVER any sailor or yakuza or mafia ink. This rule overrides any tattooed character that might appear in any reference image — IGNORE any tattoo or decorative skin/sleeve marking visible in the reference. Face: perfectly circular, 100% pure white (#FFFFFF), completely flat, no shading, no skin tone, NO nose, NO cheeks, NO visible teeth. Features: only 2 small black dot eyes, 2 short straight thin black eyebrow strokes, 1 short horizontal black mouth line. Hair: jet black, short, neat, modest 'discreet tech founder' cut — clean side-part with the fringe combed slightly to the side, NEVER long, NEVER messy, NEVER slick-back pompadour, NEVER shaved sides, NEVER a buzzcut, NEVER pomade-shiny. NO beard, NO moustache, NO sideburns. Hands: pure white MITTEN shapes with no individual finger detail. WARDROBE — TWO MODES, the per-scene prompt picks one: (A) HOME CASUAL (early scenes / late-night kitchen / Belleville flashback): plain white short-sleeve t-shirt OR plain medium-grey long-sleeve sweatshirt, plain dark indigo jeans, plain white sneakers OR bare feet at home. NO logos. NO patterns. NO branding. (B) NEW WEALTH UNIFORM (post-week-one scenes — property, lawyers, philanthropy meetings, Geneva dinner, jet, court, charity, business): single-breasted dark anthracite-grey wool suit (NEVER black, NEVER navy, NEVER pinstripe, NEVER three-piece, NEVER double-breasted), plain white cotton dress shirt with the top button undone (NEVER any tie, NEVER any bowtie, NEVER any pocket square), polished plain black leather oxford shoes. ACCESSORIES (always-on, both modes): a single discreet flat matte-black wristwatch on the RIGHT wrist (small black square shape with a thin black strap, no gold, no logos visible). NEVER a pinky ring. NEVER a gold chain. NEVER cufflinks. NEVER a fedora or any hat. NEVER glasses. NEVER a cigar. NEVER a tattoo. NEVER an earring. The black wristwatch on the right mitten is the ONLY recurring accessory and serves as the silent identity marker. RECURRING PROP-OBJECTS that frequently appear in the protagonist's mitten hand or in the foreground (NOT clothing — these are story-anchors): a plain modern black smartphone with a glowing white-and-black bank-balance screen showing a long row of digits; a plain clear water glass filled from the kitchen tap; a single yaourt pot from a French supermarket (small white plastic pot with a thin foil lid); a plain clear champagne flute; a folded crisp white paper cheque. Body posture is contained, shoulders slightly forward, often turned partly away from camera, often seen FROM BEHIND or in PROFILE rather than full-front — the script is in second-person 'tu' and the protagonist is meant to feel like an observer of his own life. CORE IDENTITY (white round flat face, jet-black short discreet side-part hair, anthracite-grey suit OR home-casual depending on mode, no tie ever, single matte-black wristwatch on right wrist, NO ostentatious jewelry, NEVER any mafia/yakuza marker) IS IMMUTABLE.
character_block: The central subject is the new French trillionaire (see identity card above — DO NOT deviate). ABSOLUTE RULE FOR OTHER HUMANS IN THE SCENE: EVERY other human character — his younger sister (a SVT teacher in Tours), her husband, her two children, his elderly mother, his best friend, two paparazzi photographers, three Parisian tax lawyers in their XVIe-arrondissement office, the British butler, the Lyon two-Michelin-star female chef, the male sommelier, the head gardener and his four apprentices, the wardrobe woman, the agenda woman, the email woman, the fifteen ex-GIGN security operators, the Italian shipyard manager from Viareggio, the Café de Flore waiters and customers, the photographer waiting outside, the crying woman in the Monoprix yogurt aisle, the middle-aged Parisian newspaper readers, the airport ground crew and the private-jet pilot, the cybersecurity consultants who previously worked for a Qatari sovereign fund, the journalist for the long-form magazine, the African president on the speakerphone, the Aspen real-estate agent, the family-office bankers, the seven food-bank charity executives, the children's-hospital director, three judges, two TV studio anchors, the established Geneva billionaire (older, third-generation European-finance heir), the Belleville neighbours (a woman singing while doing dishes, a man shouting at his son, a barking dog called the Dupont dog), and the 28-year-old guy in the Belleville kitchen at the very end of the script — MUST share the white round featureless face style: pure WHITE ROUND FACE with only tiny black dot eyes, thin eyebrow strokes, and minimal straight mouth line. NO realistic human faces, NO skin tones, NO noses, NO cheeks, NO wrinkles, NO visible teeth. Hands always pure white mitten shapes. Secondary characters differ ONLY by: hair shape (short side-part, balding crown drawn as a smaller hair shape with white scalp showing for older men, woman's bun or bob or chignon, low grey bun for the elderly mother, salt-and-pepper slick-back for the Geneva billionaire only, military buzzcut for the security operators, longer wavy bob for the sister), small attached beard or moustache shapes (only for older men, drawn flat black or grey, NEVER on the protagonist), body build, and clothing. KEY CHARACTER MARKERS: SISTER (Tours, SVT teacher) — shoulder-length wavy dark-brown bob hair, simple beige cardigan over a plain white shirt and dark jeans, small plain wedding band on the left mitten, no makeup, slightly tired posture; ELDERLY MOTHER — small frail woman, low silver-grey bun, faded mauve cardigan over a soft white blouse, hands folded in her lap, often seated; BEST FRIEND — early thirties, short curly dark hair, plain navy hoodie over a white t-shirt, casual; JOURNALISTS / PAPARAZZI — beige trench coat over jeans, short messy hair, holding a heavy black DSLR camera with a long lens (drawn as a flat black cylindrical shape), or a small black phone-recorder; PARISIAN TAX LAWYERS (XVIe arrondissement office) — three older men in identical sober dark-charcoal three-piece suits, white shirts, plain dark-grey ties, balding crowns, tortoiseshell glasses drawn as 2 small brown ovals over the dot eyes, leather portfolios in mitten hands, seated around a polished walnut conference table; BRITISH BUTLER — older man, balding crown with thin grey side hair, formal black tailcoat over white shirt and grey waistcoat, white gloves drawn as bright bone-white mittens with a clear cuff line, perfectly upright posture; CHEF (two-Michelin-star Lyon woman) — early forties woman, hair tied back in a tight low bun, double-breasted plain white chef jacket with stand collar, plain white apron, pale blue trousers, holding a plain stainless-steel pot or a small notebook; SECURITY OPERATORS (ex-GIGN) — military buzzcut, plain black tactical polo shirt OR plain charcoal suit, discreet curly black earpiece wire visible at the ear, small black holster bulge under the jacket, sunglasses sometimes drawn as 2 small black squares over the dot eyes; CRYING WOMAN AT MONOPRIX — middle-aged woman, dark-brown shoulder-length hair, plain dark-blue raincoat over a beige sweater, holding a small black handbag, single tear drawn as a tiny blue droplet on the white round face; CAFÉ DE FLORE PATRONS — thirty-somethings in plain black or beige trench coats and turtlenecks, holding tiny espresso cups, one always raising a smartphone to film; GENEVA BILLIONAIRE (older third-generation finance heir) — sixties man, fully steel-grey clean side-part hair, immaculate dark-charcoal three-piece suit with white shirt and a plain dark-burgundy silk tie (the ONLY tie allowed in any scene of this video, worn by HIM and not the protagonist), small dark-grey pocket square folded flat, calm motionless seated posture, wineglass in mitten hand; FAMILY-OFFICE BANKERS — late thirties, plain navy suits, white shirts, plain navy or grey ties, slim laptops, sober expressions; AFRICAN PRESIDENT (on speakerphone) — appears only as a faceless figure on a screen in a video call OR as a flat black phone-handset shape in profile, never present in person; ASPEN REAL-ESTATE AGENT — woman in early forties, sleek blonde bob, plain cream blazer over a white shirt, holding a glossy property brochure; CHARITY EXECUTIVES — late forties men or women in plain dark blazers and sober shirts, charity-logo lanyards drawn as a tiny coloured rectangle on the chest, holding leather folders; HOSPITAL DIRECTOR — older man, balding crown, plain white doctor's coat over a grey suit, stethoscope drawn as a thin black tube around the neck (decorative only); BELLEVILLE NEIGHBOURS — modest ordinary-looking French people, the singing woman in a plain blue apron over a flowery dress holding a sponge, the shouting father in a plain white t-shirt and navy work trousers, the small black-and-white silhouette of the Dupont dog drawn as a flat black-and-white cartoon shape; YOUNG GUY AT THE VERY END (28 in his Belleville kitchen) — same hair shape as the protagonist but slightly messier, plain grey hoodie over a white t-shirt, plain dark jeans, holding a black phone in one mitten and an empty kettle in the other, calm content posture by an open window. The WHITE ROUND FACE is non-negotiable on every humanoid in the frame. ABSOLUTE TATTOO BAN ON EVERY CHARACTER WITHOUT EXCEPTION: NO tattoo, NO ink, NO tribal pattern, NO sleeve tattoo, NO neck tattoo, NO chest tattoo, NO back tattoo, NO forearm pattern, NO body art, NO henna, NO decorative ink-style motif on the skin or clothing of ANY person in ANY scene of this video — protagonist, sister, mother, butler, chef, security operators, lawyers, paparazzi, Geneva billionaire, Belleville neighbours, the final 28-year-old, ALL of them have plain pure-white mitten hands and plain solid-colour long-sleeve clothing with NO decorative pattern on the sleeves or arms. Arms are either fully covered by long sleeves OR rendered as plain pure-white skin — NEVER any tattoo, tribal pattern, ink, body art, or decorative motif on the arms or forearms. This rule strictly overrides any tattooed character that might appear in any reference image — if the reference shows tattoos, IGNORE them and render the new character with plain pure-white skin only. NEVER apply any mafia, yakuza, sicilian, samurai, irezumi, or cosplay marker to ANY character in this video.
global_style_card: STYLE (LOCKED, IDENTICAL IN EVERY SCENE): children's picture book / storytime YouTube animation, soft 2D cel-shading. Pure 2D — NO 3D render, NO photorealism, NO photographic image, NO photo, NO realistic skin texture, NO anime, NO manga screentones, NO chibi. Outlines: uniform, bold black, ~3-5px thickness everywhere (characters, suits, props, architecture, vehicles, smartphones, glassware, sculpted hedges, marble fireplaces, hospital beds, jet engines, wine bottles). Colors: flat fills with at most 2-3 tonal shades per object (no smooth gradients on faces or bodies). PALETTE — 'cold quiet luxury' (the look of having too much): polished marble white (Parisian apartment walls, Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat villa interiors, hospital corridors), deep matte black (the protagonist's anthracite-grey suit reads almost black, lacquered grand pianos, limousine bodies, the wristwatch, smartphones), pale champagne gold (subtle accents only on door handles, picture frames, wine glasses, light fixtures — NEVER ostentatious gold), wet midnight blue (rainy Paris streets at night, Geneva winter sky, jet windows over the Alps), winter grey-blue (Geneva snow rooftops, Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat sea on overcast days), warm Mediterranean ochre and terracotta (Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat villa exterior walls, Côte d'Azur sunset cliffs, the cypress-lined driveway), olive-green and deep cypress (the property's twelve-hectare garden hedges and lawn), French-supermarket bright mint-green and Carrefour-red (used SPARINGLY, only in the yogurt-aisle Monoprix scene and on the small Carrefour yogurt-pot prop — nothing else in the video carries supermarket colours), Café de Flore burgundy-and-cream awnings, faded raspberry-red Parisian café banquettes, soft cream linen (private-jet interior, family-office leather sofas, Aspen chalet skin), warm amber kitchen tungsten (tiny old Belleville studio kitchen at the end, the protagonist's late-night kitchen scenes — the ONLY genuinely warm light in the whole video, used as a sad ironic contrast), cold flat clinical LED-white (the trillionaire's huge new-villa kitchen at 3am, the hospital, the FBI-style cybersecurity room, the tax lawyers' Parisian office), pale ash grey (the property's high concrete-and-stone perimeter walls, the prison-like security gatehouse, the courtroom). Lighting: cinematic and oppressive — the new wealthy locations always feel COLD, EMPTY, OVER-LIT (huge LED-white kitchen at 3am, marble corridors echoing, fluorescent meeting rooms), while the protagonist's old life (Belleville studio, the very last scene, the late-night tap-water glass) is small and amber-warm. Faces stay perfectly flat white in all lighting conditions, only background and clothing react to light — NEVER use harsh rim light or gradient on faces. The phone-screen always emits a soft cold-white glow on the surrounding wall but NOT on the white face. ARCHITECTURE AND ENVIRONMENT (specific to this script — re-use these locations across scenes): a modest Parisian apartment kitchen with a basic refrigerator, a plain wooden table, a single window onto a Parisian zinc-roof courtyard, a fridge magnet from Carrefour; a Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat hilltop property with twelve hectares of manicured cypress-lined gardens, a long gravel driveway, a high stone perimeter wall with a single wrought-iron gate, an outdoor swimming pool reflecting the Mediterranean, a marble-floored entrance hall with a sweeping staircase, a fifteen-bedroom corridor with identical closed white doors, a sixty-seat private cinema with cream leather chairs, an indoor swimming pool with cold turquoise water, a wine cellar with one thousand three hundred dusty bottles in dark wooden racks, a wood-panelled library with four thousand untouched leather-bound books, a vast white-marble kitchen lit by harsh LED panels at 3am with a single tap glass of water in the foreground; a Parisian XVIe-arrondissement law-firm office with dark walnut panelling, a long polished conference table, a tall window onto Avenue Foch; a Café de Flore corner of mirrors, brass rails and burgundy banquettes, with one paparazzo silhouette outside the glass; a Monoprix yogurt aisle in the protagonist's old neighbourhood — bright fluorescent strip lights, mint-green and red dairy packaging on the shelves, a single crying secondary character; a private hangar at Le Bourget with a sleek white private jet; a private jet cabin interior with cream leather armchairs and small round porthole windows over the Alps; a Geneva private restaurant in a discreet wood-panelled room with a long single table for six and tall white candles; a children's-hospital corridor with pale-green walls and a single seated mother; a vast cypress-flanked garden of the property at dawn with the protagonist tiny in the distance; a sleeping master bedroom suite with floor-to-ceiling windows onto the dark sea at 3am, the protagonist sitting on the edge of the bed; a courthouse entrance with stone steps and a French-republic flag; finally, by contrast, a tiny 14-square-metre Belleville studio kitchen with a small open window onto an inner courtyard, a cheap modest table, a kettle on the hob, sunlight in warm amber tones. Aspect ratio: 16:9 cinematic. COMPOSITION: environment dominates the frame, the protagonist often small, often seen FROM BEHIND or in PROFILE rather than full-front (because the narration is in 'tu' — the viewer is meant to inhabit him), staring at his phone, walking down a long marble corridor with his back to camera, sitting alone at the end of a vast dining table, standing alone before a panoramic window, kneeling at the foot of an empty staircase. Tighter framing only for emphasis (the smartphone screen showing the bank balance, the tap glass of water, the Carrefour yogurt pot, the British butler bowing, the wristwatch on the right mitten). Recurring visual motifs to weave throughout: a smartphone glowing white-cold in a white mitten hand showing a long line of digits, a clear glass of water on a marble counter, a single small Carrefour yogurt pot with thin foil lid, a vast empty corridor with closed identical white doors, the manicured cypress-lined gravel driveway, a private jet wing above the Alps, a single tear-droplet on a secondary character's white face, the protagonist tiny against vast architecture, and at the very end the warm amber Belleville window contrasting with everything before.
style_block: clean 2D cel-shading storytime YouTube animation style, bold thick black outlines, flat simple colors, contemporary French quiet-luxury aesthetic, polished marble white and deep matte black with cold clinical LED light and pale champagne gold accents, Mediterranean ochre and cypress green for the Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat property, soft amber tungsten reserved for nostalgic Belleville and late-night kitchen scenes, cinematic cold composition emphasising architecture over the small protagonist, 16:9
